name: Web Probe Capturer
description: Playwright-based page capture module (Phase 2). Visits nodes in the site graph with a real browser, intercepts network resources, captures console messages, extracts page metadata (title, text, links), collects cookies and forms, measures timing metrics (TTFB, DOM content loaded, load event), and optionally takes full-page screenshots. Supports both authenticated and anonymous contexts.

types.name: ConsoleMessageLevel
types.description: Console message severity level (imported from webprobe.models)

functions.name: _map_resource_type
functions.description: Maps Playwright's resource type string to the internal ResourceType enum. Returns ResourceType.other for unknown types.
postconditions: Returns a valid ResourceType enum value
Unknown types map to ResourceType.other

functions.name: _map_console_level
functions.description: Maps console message type string to the internal ConsoleMessageLevel enum. Returns ConsoleMessageLevel.log for unknown types.
postconditions: Returns a valid ConsoleMessageLevel enum value
Unknown types map to ConsoleMessageLevel.log

functions.name: _screenshot_path
functions.description: Generates a relative screenshot file path from URL and authentication context. Converts URL path to filename (_index for root, _<path> for others), URL-encodes query strings, and organizes by auth context subdirectory.
postconditions: Returns path in format: screenshots/{auth_context.value}/{sanitized_url}.png
Root path (/) becomes '_index.png'
Path segments are joined with underscores
Query strings are URL-encoded and appended

functions.name: _visit_node
functions.description: Visits a single node with Playwright browser, capturing all page data: HTTP response, timing metrics, resources, console messages, cookies, forms, links, and screenshot. Handles both authenticated and anonymous contexts. Uses semaphore for concurrency control.

inputs.name: pool
inputs.description: Browser pool for managing Playwright contexts

inputs.name: node
inputs.description: The node to visit from the site graph

error_cases.name: javascript_evaluation_error
error_cases.condition: JavaScript evaluation fails (performance timing, page text, links, forms, or cookies)

error_data.result: affected fields set to default values (empty list/string/None)

error_cases.name: response_body_error
error_cases.condition: Fetching response body fails in on_response handler

error_data.result: size_bytes set to None for that resource

preconditions: run_dir exists or can be created
postconditions: Returns a complete NodeCapture object with all captured data
Browser context is closed even on error
Screenshot file is created if config.capture.screenshot is True and capture succeeds
Resources list contains all intercepted HTTP responses
Console messages contain all browser console output during page load
side_effects: Creates a new browser context and page
Creates screenshot directory and file if screenshot is enabled
Executes JavaScript in the page context to gather performance timing, links, and forms

structured_side_effects.description: Navigates to the URL with Playwright
Writes PNG screenshot if config.capture.screenshot is True
Creates and modifies browser context state

performance_budget.complexity: O(1) per node + O(n) for n resources/forms/cookies

functions.name: on_response
functions.description: Async event handler (nested function within _visit_node) that intercepts HTTP responses during page navigation. Extracts resource metadata including URL, type, status, size, and timing, and appends to the resources list.

inputs.name: response
inputs.description: Playwright Response object from intercepted HTTP request

error_cases.name: body_fetch_error
error_cases.condition: response.body() raises an exception or response is not OK

preconditions: response is a valid Playwright Response object
resources list is accessible in closure scope
postconditions: A Resource object is appended to the resources list
Resource contains URL, type, status code, size (if available), mime type, and timing data
side_effects: Mutates the resources list by appending a new Resource object
Fetches response body to determine size

structured_side_effects.description: Appends Resource object to captured resources
Fetches response body to calculate size

functions.name: on_console
functions.description: Synchronous event handler (nested function within _visit_node) that intercepts browser console messages during page navigation. Maps message level and appends to console_messages list.

inputs.name: msg
inputs.description: Playwright console message object with type and text attributes

preconditions: console_messages list is accessible in closure scope
node.state.url is accessible in closure scope
postconditions: A ConsoleMessage object is appended to the console_messages list
Message level is mapped via _map_console_level
side_effects: Mutates the console_messages list by appending a new ConsoleMessage object

functions.name: capture_site
functions.description: Phase 2 entry point: orchestrates visiting all nodes in the site graph with Playwright. Creates concurrent tasks for each (node, auth_context) pair, gathers results, attaches captures to nodes, verifies edges based on outgoing links, and returns updated graph with phase status.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Configuration containing capture settings (concurrency, timeout, screenshot flag, auth settings)

inputs.name: graph
inputs.description: Site graph from Phase 1 with nodes and edges to capture

inputs.name: run_dir
inputs.description: Run directory for storing screenshots and artifacts

error_cases.name: visit_node_exception
error_cases.condition: _visit_node raises an exception for a specific node

error_data.result: Creates minimal NodeCapture with error message in page_text, capture still appended to node

preconditions: graph.nodes contains valid Node objects with state.url and auth_contexts_available
postconditions: Every node in graph has captures appended for each auth context
Edges are marked as verified if target URL appears in source node's outgoing links
PhaseStatus.status is 'completed'
PhaseStatus contains timing information (started_at, completed_at, duration_ms)
Exception results are converted to minimal NodeCapture objects with error text
side_effects: Spawns concurrent async tasks (up to config.capture.concurrency)
Mutates graph.edges by setting verified flags
Creates screenshot files if config.capture.screenshot is True

structured_side_effects.description: Appends captures to nodes and sets edge verification flags
Visits each node URL with Playwright
Creates screenshot files for each captured page

performance_budget.complexity: O(n*m) where n=number of nodes, m=auth contexts per node

invariants: _RESOURCE_TYPE_MAP is a constant dictionary mapping Playwright resource types to internal ResourceType enum
All unknown resource types map to ResourceType.other
All unknown console message types map to ConsoleMessageLevel.log
Resource timing duration_ms is always set to 0 in on_response handler (placeholder for future performance API integration)
All exceptions during page capture are caught and handled gracefully with default values
Browser contexts are always closed in finally block to prevent resource leaks
Phase 2 always completes successfully (exceptions converted to error captures)

data_access.rationale: This module performs browser automation and file I/O for screenshots. It reads configuration, mutates the site graph object, and writes screenshot files.

side_effects.rationale: Navigates to URLs with Playwright browser
Writes PNG screenshots to run directory
Appends capture results to nodes and updates edge verification status
